seo.title: AI Agent Observability in Amazon Bedrock Agents Agents
seo.description: How to detect and fix ai agent observability in Amazon Bedrock Agents agents. Framework-specific detection, fixes, and Vex automation.

content.intro: AI Agent Observability in Amazon Bedrock Agents is critical due to its fully managed architecture spanning action groups, knowledge bases, and guardrails. Problems manifest as malformed Lambda responses breaking agent orchestration, knowledge base chunking missing key documents, guardrails failing domain policies, and session state loss across multi-turn invocations. These issues silently degrade enterprise deployments, requiring deep integration with Bedrock's invoke_agent() API for reliable monitoring.

howItManifests.scenario: Action group Lambda functions returning malformed responses
howItManifests.symptom: Agent returns 'ActionFailed' with parse errors in trace events
howItManifests.impact: Breaks agent orchestration, causing infinite loops or fallback to default responses

howItManifests.scenario: Knowledge base retrieval missing relevant documents due to chunking
howItManifests.symptom: retrievalMetadata shows low relevance scores despite relevant content existing
howItManifests.impact: Agent hallucinates answers or provides incomplete responses

howItManifests.scenario: Agent guardrails not catching domain-specific policy violations
howItManifests.symptom: guardrailConfigApplied=true but contentAssessment shows ALLOWED for prohibited queries
howItManifests.impact: Regulatory compliance violations and security exposures

howItManifests.scenario: Session state loss between multi-turn agent invocations
howItManifests.symptom: sessionState.sessionAttributes empty or inconsistent across invoke_agent() calls
howItManifests.impact: Context loss causes repetitive questions and poor user experience

detection.name: Action Group Response Validation
detection.description: Parse Bedrock trace events for malformed action group responses

detection.name: Knowledge Base Retrieval Quality Check
detection.description: Monitor retrieval metadata for low relevance scores indicating chunking issues

detection.name: Guardrail Violation Detection
detection.description: Check guardrail assessments against domain-specific policies

fixes.name: Standardized Action Group Response Wrapper
fixes.description: Lambda wrapper ensuring Bedrock-compatible action responses

fixes.name: Enhanced Knowledge Base Chunking
fixes.description: Multi-size chunking strategy for better retrieval coverage

fixes.name: Custom Domain Guardrails
fixes.description: Layered guardrails with custom content policies

vexAutomation.heading: Automating AI Agent Observability Detection with Vex
vexAutomation.description:
Vex provides end-to-end observability for Bedrock Agents by wrapping invoke_agent() calls with automated verification. It detects malformed responses, retrieval failures, guardrail bypasses, and session drift in real-time, providing structured error reports with remediation paths. The platform integrates seamlessly via vex.verify() post-processing, enabling automatic alerting, dashboarding, and remediation workflows for enterprise-scale deployments.

Vex's Bedrock-specific detectors parse native trace events and sessionState, mapping failures to actionable insights. Integration requires only pip install boto3 vex-sdk and wrapping existing agent invocations. Automated remediation templates fix 80% of common issues without code changes.

Production deployments see 95% faster MTTR with Vex's ML-powered anomaly detection that learns your agent's normal behavior patterns.
vexAutomation.code:
import boto3
import vex

from vex.integrations.bedrock import BedrockAgentVerifier

# Initialize Bedrock client
bedrock = boto3.client('bedrock-agent-runtime')

# Vex Bedrock verifier
verifier = BedrockAgentVerifier()

@verifier.observe()
def invoke_monitored_agent(agent_id, session_id, input_text):
    response = bedrock.invoke_agent(
        agentId=agent_id,
        agentAliasId='latest',
        sessionId=session_id,
        inputText=input_text,
        enableTrace=True
    )
    
    # Vex auto-verifies response
    issues = vex.verify(response)
    
    if issues:
        vex.alert({
            'agentId': agent_id,
            'issues': issues,
            'sessionId': session_id
        })
    
    return response

# Usage
result = invoke_monitored_agent('AGENT123', 'sess-456', 'What is our Q4 revenue?')

cta.heading: Detect and Fix This Automatically with Vex
